COMPLAINT FOR DAMAGES-SEXUAL HARASSMENT
CIVIL COVER SHEET RECEIVED
AMENDED COMPLAINT
REPLY MEMORANDUM IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY DEFT MARTIN T. RESENDEZ Y GUAJARDO

DEMURRER LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER CONTINUED FROM DEC-14-2001 TO LAW AND MOTION AT JAN-23-2002, 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 ON THE COURT'S OWN MOTION. REPORTER: JOE VICKSTEIN. HEARING SET FOR JAN-23-2002 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, DEMURRER - NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING -- DEFENDANT GUAJARDO'S DEMURRER -- DEMURRER AS TO CAUSES OF ACTION 1 AND 2 FOR SEXUAL HARASSMENT AND GENDER DISCRIMINATION -- SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND WITH RESPECT TO THE UNRUH CIVIL RIGHTS ACT CLAIM. AS TO SEXUAL HARASSMENT AND GENDER DISCRIMINATION SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO SPECIFICY THE BASIS FOR RELIEF AND THE SUPPORTIVE STATUTORY AND CONSTITUTIONAL PROVISIONS. RE CAUSES OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS AND NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS -- OVERRULED WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO DEMURRING TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. ASSAULT AND BATTERY CAUSES OF ACTION OVERRULED. 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. HON. JAMES J. MCBRIDE. REPORTED BY ERIC THRONE. CSR 7855 (GPS)

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-14-2002 Continued To Apr-05-02 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

DEMURRER
Off Calendar Jan-23-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling -- defendant GUAJARDO's demurrer -- Demurrer as to Causes of Action 1 and 2 for sexual harassment and gender discrimination -- sustained without leave to amend with respect to the Unruh Civil Rights Act claim. As to sexual harassment and gender discrimination sustained with leave to amend to specificy the basis for relief and the supportive statutory and constitutional provisions. Re causes of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress -- overruled without prejudice to demurring to the amended complaint. Assault and battery causes of action overruled. 10 days leave to amend. (JJM/cl)
